items.title: S&P 500 Sell-Off: Why Warren Buffett Is Still Right About Index Investing, Even After Selling His
items.author: Reuben Gregg Brewer
items.description: Despite Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway selling its S&P 500 index fund positions, the author argues that Buffett's advice to buy and hold an index fund is still valid. The article discusses the long-term performance of the S&P 500 index and how investors can fine-tune the strategy to fit their needs, such as by choosing a dividend-focused ETF.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article mentions that Berkshire Hathaway sold its position in the SPDR S&P 500 Trust ETF, but notes that this position was less than 0.01% of Berkshire's portfolio, so it was not a material investment decision.

Similar to the SPDR S&P 500 Trust ETF, the article mentions that Berkshire Hathaway sold its position in the Vanguard S&P 500 ETF, but this was also a small position that was not a significant part of Berkshire's investment strategy.

The article suggests that investors can fine-tune Buffett's index investing advice by choosing a dividend-focused ETF like the Schwab U.S. Dividend Equity ETF, which has a higher yield than the S&P 500 index and has also seen its share price rise over time.

items.title: US Stocks Likely To Open Lower As Futures Decline: 'Stay Calm And Stick With Your Long-Term Plan,' Says Expert Amid Correction Woes
items.author: Rishabh Mishra
items.description: U.S. stock futures declined as benchmark indices hovered around the correction zone. Experts advised investors to stay calm and stick to their long-term plans, as market pullbacks are common and a complete recovery is inevitable over time.
